payload.delta: The
background question task completed. Let me read
the output file to see the answers.
Your
answers
came in — let me read them.
The
output shows the question was cancelled: `
{"answers":{"cancelled":true,"reason":"
turn_ended"}}`. So the user didn't actually
answer — the question was dismissed/cancelled,
likely because the turn ended. Per the tool guidance
: "if `answers` is empty and a
`note` says the user dismissed it, they
chose not to answer — do not treat this as
selecting the recommended option; decide based on context and

do not re-ask the same question."

So I
should report plainly: the questions weren't answered — they
were cancelled (turn_ended). I shouldn't re
-ask. The user's original request was just to
ask the three questions and end the turn — which
I did. The mechanism worked as requested; the
answers just never came because the turn ending cancelled the
question card. I can note this briefly and candid
ly.
The
background-question mechanism worked as
requested — the card was posted and the completion notification
arrived — but the payload shows no answers: `
{"cancelled":true,"reason":"turn_ended"}`.
Ending my turn right after asking cancelled the question

before you could respond to it.

So the test
result: `AskUserQuestion` with `background=true` does
deliver a completion notification, but in this case the
question was voided by the turn ending rather than
answered. If you want to actually answer them,
I can re-ask the same three questions in
the foreground — otherwise I'll leave it here.
